session.assistant: Let me check the Graphite integration documentation:
session.tool_result:
1→# Graphite Integration 2→ 3→An optional enhancement that enables stacked PR workflows when using erk. 4→ 5→## Do You Need This? 6→ 7→**Only if you already use Graphite.** This tutorial assumes you're familiar with [Graphite](https://graphite.dev) and understand stacking concepts (`gt create`, `gt submit`, upstack/downstack). 8→ 9→If you're new to stacked PRs, visit [graphite.dev](https://graphite.dev) first. 10→ 11→**Value prop:** When Graphite is detected, erk uses `gt` under the hood for branch creation and PR submission. Your existing Graphite workflow gets enhanced with plan-driven implementation. 12→ 13→## Setup 14→ 15→### Verify Graphite is Installed 16→ 17→```bash 18→gt --version 19→``` 20→ 21→If not installed, see [graphite.dev/docs/getting-started](https://graphite.dev/docs/getting-started). 22→ 23→### No Additional Configuration Needed 24→ 25→`erk` auto-detects Graphite. If `gt` is on your PATH, erk uses it automatically. 26→ 27→To verify Graphite is installed and detected: 28→ 29→```bash 30→erk doctor

session.assistant:
The output shows that **Graphite CLI (gt) is not installed** in this environment. The `gt submit --no-interactive` command cannot be executed because the Graphite CLI tool is not available.
## Summary
**Command attempted:** `gt submit --no-interactive`
**Result:** FAILED - Command not found (exit code 127)
**Root cause:** Graphite CLI (`gt`) is not installed in this environment.
**Evidence from `erk doctor`:** ``` ℹ️ Graphite CLI (gt) not installed ```
The documentation indicates that Graphite is an optional enhancement for stacked PR workflows. To use `gt submit`, Graphite would need to be installed first via the installation instructions at [graphite.dev/docs/getting-started](https://graphite.dev/docs/getting-started).
If you'd like to proceed with PR submission using the standard erk workflow (without Graphite), you can use `erk pr submit` instead, which uses GitHub CLI directly.
